nodes.x: Therefore leaving the principles of the doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto perfection; not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works, and of faith toward God,

“ Therefore leaving the principles of tho doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto perfection ; not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works, and of faith toward God, of tho doctrine of baptisms, and of laying on of hands, and of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment. And this will we do, if God permit. For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and havo…
Of the doctrine of baptisms, and of laying on of hands, and of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.

And when Paul had laid his hands upon them, the Holy Ghost came on them; and they spake with tongues and prophesied. YVe discover by these, the doctrine of the laying on of the hands. — And for the doctrine of the resurrection of the dead and of eternal judgment: Hebrews Gth chap. 2^d verse, of the doctrine of baptism, and of laying on of the hands, and of reserrection of the dead.andof eternal…
And this will we do, if God permit.
For that ye ought to say, If the Lord will, we shall live, and do this, or that.
God help us to do right and preserve our purity, keep his laws and lead us in the paths of life, that while we live upon the earth we may operate with him in the salvation of
And this shall be our covenant—that we will walk in all the ordinances of the Lord.
For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,

first seemingly strange, we saw that everything was plain, fair and honorable. In doing what they required, we should only do, in fact, what, as true-hearted believers in the ancient Gospel, we ought to do; and if we failed to receive the promised blessing, and thereby proved the Elders' testimony false, our religious condition would, nevertheless, be then as good as other Christians, and a…
And have tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the world to come,
And hast made us unto our God kings and priests: and we shall reign on the earth.
But that every man might speak in the name of God the Lord, even the Savior of the world;
And I, Jesus Christ, your Lord and your God, have spoken it.
¶ Behold, it is come, and it is done, saith the Lord God; this is the day whereof I have spoken.
If they shall fall away, to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they crucify to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put him to an open shame.

And then shall they rejoice; for they shall know that it is a blessing unto them from the hand of God; and their scales of darkness shall begin to fall from their eyes; and many generations shall not pass away among them, save they shall be a pure and a delightsome people.
And they that believe not unto eternal damnation; for they cannot be redeemed from their spiritual fall, because they repent not;

And they shall cry from the dust; yea, even repentance unto their brethren, even after many generations have gone by them. And it shall come to pass that their cry shall go, even according to the simpleness of their words.
Have mercy, O Lord, upon the wicked mob, who have driven thy people, that they may cease to spoil, that they may repent of their sins if repentance is to be found;
For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs meet for them by whom it is dressed, receiveth blessing from God:

For as the rain cometh down, and the snow from heaven, and returneth not thither, but watereth the earth, and maketh it bring forth and bud, that it may give seed to the sower, and bread to the eater:
Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:
4. Worship the God of heaven, Who causeth the rain and the dew to descend on the earth, 3 And doeth everything upon the earth,
Ask ye of the Lord rain in the time of the latter rain; so the Lord shall make bright clouds, and give them showers of rain, to every one grass in the field.
But that which beareth thorns and briers is rejected, and is nigh unto cursing; whose end is to be burned.
¶ For wickedness burneth as the fire: it shall devour the briers and thorns, and shall kindle in the thickets of the forest, and they shall mount up like the lifting up of smoke.
For wickedness burneth as the fire; it shall devour the briers and thorns, and shall kindle in the thickets of the forests, and they shall mount up like the lifting up of smoke.
And the light of Israel shall be for a fire, and his Holy One for a flame: and it shall burn and devour his thorns and his briers in one day;

A Crown of Thorns, a Crown of Glory By Elder James E. Faust Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles My beloved brothers and sisters and friends, today I wish to speak of thorns, briars, slivers, and a crown of thorns. I also wish to speak of the exquisite beauty and fragrance to be found in life, and of a crown of glory. I wish that I better understood all of the divine purposes in having to contend…
¶ For thus saith the Lord to the men of Judah and Jerusalem, Break up your fallow ground, and sow not among thorns.
But, beloved, we are persuaded better things of you, and things that accompany salvation, though we thus speak.

Beloved, when I gave all diligence to write unto you of the common salvation, it was needful for me to write unto you, and exhort you that ye should earnestly contend for the faith which was once delivered unto the saints.
Beloved, if our heart condemn us not, then have we confidence toward God.

But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth:
For if our heart condemn us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.
For God is not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love, which ye have shewed toward his name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister.
For as touching the ministering to the saints, it is superfluous for me to write to you:
And we desire that every one of you do shew the same diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end:
But we will give ourselves continually to prayer, and to the ministry of the word.
That ye be not slothful, but followers of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises.
The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute.
He that is slothful shall not be counted worthy to stand, and he that learns not his duty and shows himself not approved shall not be counted worthy to stand. Even so. Amen.
The way of the slothful man is as an hedge of thorns: but the way of the righteous is made plain.
But he that doeth not anything until he is commanded, and receiveth a commandment with doubtful heart, and keepeth it with slothfulness, the same is damned.
For when God made promise to Abraham, because he could swear by no greater, he sware by himself,

LXXII.  But  as  God  has  allotted  all  painful  things  to  the 
outward  sense  in  great  abundance  and  intensity,  so  also  has  he 
bestowed  on  the  virtuous  soul  a  boundless  store  of  good  things. 
Accordingly  he  speaks  with  reference  to  the  perfect  man 
Abraham  in  the  following  manner :  "  By  myself  have  I  sworn, 
saith  the  Lord,  that  because  thou  bast …
The oath which he sware to our father Abraham,
And Abraham said, I will swear.

They made many covenants in Abraham’s 
time, and slew victims, and offered sacrifices, 
and as yet oaths were not. Whence then did 
they come in? When evil increased, when all 
was confusion, upside down, when men had 
turned aside to idolatry: then it was, then, 
when men appeared no longer worthy to be 
believed, that they called God as witness, as if 
thereby giving an adequate surety for what…
Saying, Surely blessing I will bless thee, and multiplying I will multiply thee.

That in blessing I will bless thee, and in multiplying I will multiply thy seed as the stars of the heaven, and as the sand which is upon the sea shore; and thy seed shall possess the gate of his enemies;
And I will multiply blessings upon him, inasmuch as he will be humble before me.
And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.
I will bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
And I will make my covenant between me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.
And so, after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.
And being fully persuaded that, what he had promised, he was able also to perform.
For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.
And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.
And this is the promise that he hath promised us, even eternal life.
And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.
For men verily swear by the greater: and an oath for confirmation is to them an end of all strife.

And behold, I swear unto you, if ye will do this, with an oath, ye shall not be destroyed; but if ye will not do this, I swear unto you with an oath, that on the morrow month I will command that my armies shall come down against you, and they shall not stay their hand and shall spare not, but shall slay you, and shall let fall the sword upon you even until ye shall become extinct.
And thou shalt swear, The Lord liveth, in truth, in judgment, and in righteousness; and the nations shall bless themselves in him, and in him shall they glory.

That he who blesseth himself in the earth shall bless himself in the God of truth; and he that sweareth in the earth shall swear by the God of truth; because the former troubles are forgotten, and because they are hid from mine eyes.
But verily, verily, I say unto you, swear not at all; neither by heaven, for it is God’s throne;
Wherein God, willing more abundantly to shew unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his counsel, confirmed it by an oath:

Paul said to his Hebrew brethren, that God being more abundantly
    willing to show unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his
    counsel, he confirmed it by an oath. He also exhorts them, who,
    through faith and patience inherit the promises.
I counsel thee to keep the king’s commandment, and that in regard of the oath of God.

Therefore, he giveth this promise unto you, with an immutable covenant that they shall be fulfilled; and all things wherewith you have been afflicted shall work together for your good, and to my name’s glory, saith the Lord.
thee from iniquity, and that thou shouldst become to Him a son, and a servant, and a minister of His presence.
And in a manner that we may be found worthy, in thy sight, to secure a fulfilment of the promises which thou hast made unto us, thy people, in the revelations given unto us;
That by two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to lie, we might have a strong consolation, who have fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope set before us:
Which hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and steadfast, and which entereth into that within the veil;
And our hope of you is steadfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the sufferings, so shall ye be also of the consolation.
Which hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and steadfast, and which entereth into that within the veil;
Our soul waiteth for the Lord: he is our help and our shield.

every soul now waiting before thee, that our hearts may be united as one, and that we may approach thee in a manner acceptable in thy sight. May every emotion of our souls arise in unison unto thee in humble praise and adoration for all thy mercies unto the creatures of thy creation. We remember, our Father and our God, that we are indebted unto thee for our existence—for having been sent
Whither the forerunner is for us entered, even Jesus, made an high priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
Called of God an high priest after the order of Melchisedec.
For he testifieth, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.
¶ And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.
And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,
And it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,
